message:
refactor(core,servers,render): centralize PresetKind enum in core and eliminate duplication

This commit consolidates preset definitions from multiple crates into a single source
of truth in scriptbots-core, reducing code duplication and ensuring consistency across
REST API, MCP server, and GPUI renderer.

## Centralized PresetKind in Core (core/lib.rs +74 lines)

Added comprehensive PresetKind enum with full preset logic:

- `PresetKind::Arctic` - harsh cold environment (low food growth, temperature penalties)
- `PresetKind::BoomBust` - volatile population cycles (high food growth, rapid metabolism)
- `PresetKind::ClosedWorld` - finite resources (no respawning food, survival challenge)

Public API methods:
- `as_str()` → Convert to canonical string name ("arctic", "boom_bust", "closed_world")
- `all()` → Static slice of all available presets for enumeration
- `from_name(name)` → Parse from string with flexible aliases:
  - Accepts "boom_bust", "boombust", "boom-bust" (case-insensitive)
  - Accepts "closed_world", "closedworld", "closed-world"
- `apply_to_config(config)` → Directly mutate ScriptBotsConfig for renderer use
- `patch()` → Generate serde_json::Value for REST/MCP patch operations

## REST/MCP Server Simplification (servers.rs -25 lines)

Removed local helper functions and switched to core PresetKind:
- Deleted `preset_names()` → Use `PresetKind::all().map(|p| p.as_str())`
- Deleted `preset_patch()` → Use `PresetKind::from_name().patch()`
- REST endpoint `/api/presets` now uses centralized enum
- REST endpoint `/api/presets/apply` validates via `from_name()`
- MCP `list_presets` tool uses centralized list
- MCP `apply_preset` tool uses centralized patch generation

## GPUI Renderer Cleanup (render/lib.rs -17 lines)

Removed duplicate PresetKind definition:
- Deleted local enum with Arctic/BoomBust/ClosedWorld variants
- Deleted local `as_str()` implementation
- Imported `scriptbots_core::PresetKind` instead
- `apply_preset()` method now uses `PresetKind::apply_to_config()`

## Import Cleanup (control_cli.rs -1 line)

Removed unused serde import after preset consolidation.

## Documentation (PLAN.md)

Marked Wave Function Collapse map creator as "Currently In Progress - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23"
for MVP rule-based WFC implementation.

This refactoring ensures preset definitions stay synchronized across all surfaces (REST, MCP,
CLI, GUI) and provides a single location for adding new presets in the future. The flexible
`from_name()` parser supports both snake_case and kebab-case variants for better UX.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -464,15 +464,15 @@ Implementation notes [2025-10-23]:
 
 ---
 
-## Sandbox Map Creator (Wave Function Collapse) — Comprehensive Roadmap
+## Sandbox Map Creator (Wave Function Collapse) — Comprehensive Roadmap [Currently In Progress - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23]
 
 ### Purpose & Outcomes
 - Provide an interactive, deterministic sandbox for generating and editing world maps using Wave Function Collapse (WFC) and complementary procedural techniques.
 - Output coherent terrain/biome tiles and a fertility/food seed map that plugs directly into `scriptbots-core` (terrain, food grid, population seeding).
 - Expose REST/MCP/CLI knobs for automated sweeps; ship example tilesets and recipes for visually appealing results.
 
 ### Scope (MVP → Advanced)
-1) MVP (rule-based WFC)
+1) MVP (rule-based WFC) [Currently In Progress - GPT-5 Codex 2025-10-23]
    - Tileset-driven WFC (adjacency matrices, rotations/reflections, weights), CPU implementation.
    - Deterministic generation (seeded RNG), resolution presets (e.g., 64×64, 128×128, 256×256).
    - Export to core layers: `TerrainLayer` (enum per tile) and `fertility: Grid<f32>` (0..1); optional temperature mask.
